rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative sulfonylation-azidation of alkenes using arylsulfonyl hydrazides and TMSN3 to give β-azidoarylsulfones via radical-polar crossover
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of arylsulfonyl hydrazide to sulfonyl radical (with -3H+, -3e-, -N2); subsequent anodic oxidation of benzyl radical intermediate to benzyl cation; cathodic reduction of protons to H2
electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the arylsulfonyl hydrazide (losing 3e-, 3H+, N2) to generate a sulfonyl radical A, which adds to the styrene to give benzyl radical B; B is further oxidized at the anode to benzyl cation C, which is trapped by azide from TMSN3, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Anodic oxidation of the sulfonyl hydrazide 1a (CV shows 1a oxidizes at lower potential than 2a; TMSN3 shows no oxidation peak)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed (4aa, 0%); TEMPO-sulfonyl adduct 5 detected by HRMS ([M+H]+ 430.2392)

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)benzene
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 4ay isolated in 6% along with normal product 4ax (62%), confirming radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: alkene-tethered diol substrate 6
probe_reagents.observation: Without TMSN3, intramolecular OH nucleophile traps the cation to give ring-formed tetrahydrofuran 7 in 51%, supporting carbocation intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, 3
probe_reagents.observation: 1a oxidation peak much lower than 2a; TMSN3 shows almost no oxidation peak, indicating 1a is oxidized first at anode

notes: Carbocation intermediate C is supported by trapping with internal hydroxyl nucleophile (Scheme 2d). Strongly electron-donating (p-OMe) and electron-withdrawing (p-NO2) styrenes failed, and p-CF3 styrene gave only trace product, consistent with the need for a moderately stabilizable benzylic radical/cation. α-substituted styrenes give higher yields due to more stable tertiary benzyl cation/radical.
